source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/230915 기술문서 & 임상평가 보완 리뷰 (with 컨설팅)/mdcg_2021-24_en_0.pdf

document.title: MDCG 2021-24 Guidance on classification of medical devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: MDCG 2021-24 provides EU MDR guidance on risk-based classification of medical devices under Annex VIII.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Endorsed by the Medical Device Coordination Group; the document states it is not a European Commission document, is not legally binding, and only the Court of Justice of the European Union can give binding interpretations of Union law.
keyPoints: EU medical device classification is a risk-based system considering vulnerability of the human body and device-related risks.
MDR Article 51 divides devices into classes I, IIa, IIb and III based on intended purpose and inherent risks.
Technical documentation must include the device risk class and justification for the classification rule or rules applied.
Device class affects conformity assessment, clinical evaluation and investigation, post-market surveillance, traceability, and instructions for use.
If several classification rules or sub-rules apply, the strictest rule resulting in the higher classification applies.

entities.name: Medical Device Coordination Group
entities.evidence: This document has been endorsed by the Medical Device Coordination Group (MDCG) established by Article 103 of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

entities.name: European Commission
entities.evidence: The MDCG is composed of representatives of all Member States and it is chaired by a representative of the European Commission.

entities.name: Court of Justice of the European Union
entities.evidence: Only the Court of Justice of the European Union can give binding interpretations of Union law.

entities.name: Regulation (EU) 2017/745
entities.evidence: The classification rules are set out in Annex VIII of Regulation (EU) 2017/745 on medical devices (MDR).

entities.name: International Medical Device Regulators Forum
entities.evidence: They correspond, to a large extent, to the classification rules established by the International Medical Device Regulators Forum (IMDRF) in the guidance document GHTF/SG1/N77:2012.

entities.name: Notified body
entities.evidence: The higher the class of the device, the greater the involvement of a notified body in conformity assessment.

entities.name: Competent authority
entities.evidence: Any dispute between the manufacturer and the notified body concerned, arising from the application of Annex VIII, may be referred for a decision to the competent authority of the Member State.

concepts.name: Medical device classification
concepts.evidence: The classification of medical devices in use by the EU medical device legislation is a risk-based system.

concepts.name: Classification rules
concepts.evidence: These are referred to as the 'classification rules' and are set out in Annex VIII of Regulation (EU) 2017/745 on medical devices (MDR).

concepts.name: Device classes I, IIa, IIb and III
concepts.evidence: According to MDR Article 51 devices are divided into the following classes I, IIa, IIb and III.

concepts.name: Class I subdivisions
concepts.evidence: Class I devices can be further subdivided into Is – sterile condition, Im – measuring function and Ir – reusable surgical.

concepts.name: Technical documentation classification justification
concepts.evidence: The technical documentation to be drawn up by the manufacturer must include the risk class of the device and the justification for the classification rule(s) applied.

concepts.name: Conformity assessment
concepts.evidence: Conformity assessment is the process demonstrating whether the requirements of the MDR relating to a device have been fulfilled.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: For any device regardless of class, the manufacturer must ensure the general safety and performance requirements are satisfied. This includes carrying out a clinical evaluation.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: The manufacturer must update the clinical evaluation with clinically relevant information coming from post-market surveillance.

concepts.name: Unique Device Identifier
concepts.evidence: Devices must be assigned a Unique Device Identifier (UDI) number and be registered in the electronic system, in accordance with MDR Article 29.

concepts.name: Instructions for use
concepts.evidence: Generally, instructions for use must be supplied together with the device.

concepts.name: Duration of use
concepts.evidence: Transient: Normally intended for continuous use for less than 60 minutes. Short term: Normally intended for continuous use for between 60 minutes and 30 days. Long term: Normally intended for continuous use for more than 30 days.

concepts.name: Invasive device
concepts.evidence: Any device which, in whole or in part, penetrates inside the body, either through a body orifice or through the surface of the body.

concepts.name: Implantable device
concepts.evidence: Any device intended to be partially introduced into the human body by clinical intervention and intended to remain in place after the procedure for at least 30 days shall also be deemed to be an implantable device.

concepts.name: Active medical device
concepts.evidence: Active device means any device, the operation of which depends on a source of energy other than that generated by the human body for that purpose, or by gravity.

concepts.name: Medical device software classification
concepts.evidence: Rule 11 describes and categorizes the risk of software based on the combination of the significance of the information provided by the software to the healthcare decision and the healthcare situation or patient’s condition.

concepts.name: Systems and procedure packs
concepts.pageTarget: EU MDR Systems and Procedure Packs
concepts.evidence: System and procedure packs is described in MDR Article 22.

evidence.claim: MDCG 2021-24 is titled 'Guidance on classification of medical devices'.
evidence.evidenceText: MDCG 2021-24 Guidance on classification of medical devices October 2021

evidence.claim: The document date shown in the source is October 2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Guidance on classification of medical devices October 2021

evidence.claim: The document was endorsed by the Medical Device Coordination Group.
evidence.evidenceText: This document has been endorsed by the Medical Device Coordination Group (MDCG) established by Article 103 of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The guidance states it is not legally binding and is not a European Commission document.
evidence.evidenceText: The document is not a European Commission document and it cannot be regarded as reflecting the official position of the European Commission. Any views expressed in this document are not legally binding.

evidence.claim: EU medical device classification is risk-based.
evidence.evidenceText: The classification of medical devices in use by the EU medical device legislation is a risk-based system taking into account the vulnerability of the human body and the potential risks associated with the devices.

evidence.claim: Classification rules are set out in Annex VIII of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: These are referred to as the 'classification rules' and are set out in Annex VIII of Regulation (EU) 2017/745 on medical devices (MDR).

evidence.claim: MDR Article 51 divides devices into classes I, IIa, IIb and III.
evidence.evidenceText: According to MDR Article 51 devices are divided into the following classes I, IIa, IIb and III, taking into account the intended purpose of the devices and their inherent risks.

evidence.claim: Class I devices can be subdivided into Is, Im and Ir.
evidence.evidenceText: Class I devices can be further subdivided into Is – sterile condition, Im – measuring function and Ir – reusable surgical.

evidence.claim: Technical documentation must include device risk class and classification justification.
evidence.evidenceText: The technical documentation to be drawn up by the manufacturer must include the risk class of the device and the justification for the classification rule(s) applied in accordance with Annex VIII of the MDR.

evidence.claim: Conformity assessment demonstrates whether MDR requirements for a device have been fulfilled.
evidence.evidenceText: Conformity assessment is the process demonstrating whether the requirements of the MDR relating to a device have been fulfilled.

evidence.claim: For most device classes, conformity is assessed by a notified body, with greater involvement for higher classes.
evidence.evidenceText: Demonstrating conformity is in the first instance the responsibility of the manufacturer and for most devices classes the conformity is then assessed by a notified body. The higher the class of the device, the greater the involvement of a notified body in conformity assessment.

evidence.claim: All devices, regardless of class, must comply with relevant MDR obligations.
evidence.evidenceText: Irrespective of the class of the device, all devices must comply with all relevant obligations of the MDR.

evidence.claim: General safety and performance requirements apply to devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The devices must meet the general safety and performance requirements, including the requirements regarding the information to be supplied by the manufacturer (Annex I of the MDR).
evidence.pageTarget: MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements

evidence.claim: Devices must be CE marked except custom-made devices and devices intended for clinical investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: The devices must be CE marked (except custom-made devices and devices intended for clinical investigation).

evidence.claim: Devices must be assigned a UDI and registered in the electronic system under MDR Article 29.
evidence.evidenceText: Be assigned a Unique Device Identifier (UDI) number and be registered in the electronic system, in accordance with MDR Article 29.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is required for any device regardless of class.
evidence.evidenceText: For any device regardless of class, the manufacturer must ensure the general safety and performance requirements are satisfied. This includes carrying out a clinical evaluation.

evidence.claim: For implantable devices and class III devices, premarket clinical investigation is compulsory with specified exceptions.
evidence.evidenceText: For implantable devices and class III devices, a premarket clinical investigation is compulsory, with some exceptions.

evidence.claim: Class I devices require a post-market surveillance report available to competent authorities on request.
evidence.evidenceText: For class I devices, including sterile, those with a measuring function and the reusable surgical instruments, the manufacturer must prepare and maintain a post-market surveillance report (MDR Article 85), which must be made available to the competent authorities on request.

evidence.claim: Class IIa, IIb and III devices require a periodic safety update report.
evidence.evidenceText: For class IIa, IIb and III devices, the manufacturer must prepare a periodic safety update report for each device, and, where relevant, for each category or group of devices (MDR Article 86).

evidence.claim: The PSUR update frequency is at least annually for class IIb and III devices and at least every two years for class IIa devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This report must be updated at least annually for class IIb and III devices and at least every two years for class IIa devices.

evidence.claim: Instructions for use generally must be supplied with the device.
evidence.evidenceText: Generally, instructions for use must be supplied together with the device.

evidence.claim: Transient use means normally intended for continuous use for less than 60 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Transient Normally intended for continuous use for less than 60 minutes.

evidence.claim: Short-term use means continuous use between 60 minutes and 30 days.
evidence.evidenceText: Short term Normally intended for continuous use for between 60 minutes and 30 days.

evidence.claim: Long-term use means continuous use for more than 30 days.
evidence.evidenceText: Long term Normally intended for continuous use for more than 30 days.

evidence.claim: An invasive device penetrates inside the body through a body orifice or body surface.
evidence.evidenceText: Any device which, in whole or in part, penetrates inside the body, either through a body orifice or through the surface of the body.

evidence.claim: Software is an active device and should not be reviewed only in the context of Rule 11.
evidence.evidenceText: Software is also an active device. Software should be reviewed not only in the context of Rule 11.

evidence.claim: If multiple rules or sub-rules apply, the strictest rule producing the higher classification applies.
evidence.evidenceText: In case several rules, or if, within the same classification rule, several sub-rules, apply to the same device based on the device intended purpose, the strictest rule and sub-rule resulting in higher classification will apply.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose assigned by the manufacturer determines the device class.
evidence.evidenceText: It is the intended purpose assigned by the manufacturer to the device that determines the class of the device and not the class assigned to other similar products.

evidence.claim: Classification disputes involving a notified body may be referred to the competent authority of the Member State.
evidence.evidenceText: Any dispute between the manufacturer and the notified body concerned, arising from the application of Annex VIII, may be referred for a decision to the competent authority of the Member State.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal states MFDS/domestic, but the source text is an EU MDR MDCG guidance document.
No product models are identified in the source text despite productSignal and sourcePath references to HAD/A/F 1417-1717 and A1417MCW/A1717MCW/F1417MCW.
No revision number beyond document identifier MDCG 2021-24 is present in the source text.
Some graphical summary text appears OCR-degraded, including spelling errors such as 'pupose', 'conatct', and similar artifacts.
